chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2313 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For order on M.A No.9745/2025 (U/A) 2. For order on office objection (s) 3. For order on M.A No.9746/2025/2025 (Exemption) 4. For hearing of main case 13.01.2026 Mr.Rashid Hussain, Advocate for Petitioners ********* 1. Granted. 2to4. The primary relief sought by the petitioner in the present petition pertains to the demarcation of agricultural land admeasuring 03‐35 acres out of Survey No. 567/1, situated in Deh Mir Rukan, Taluka Qazi Ahmed, District Nawabshah. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the petitioners had moved an application for demarcation (available at page 313 of the Court file) to the Mukhtiarkar (Rev.) Qazi Ahmed, which has not been decided. Thereafter, the petitioners submitted another application before the Senior Member, Board of Revenue, Hyderabad (available at page 311 of the Court file), which also remains pending without any order. To address grievances of the nature raised in the present petition, the Government of Sindh, through Notification dated 21.10.2024, has established a “Public Grievance Redressal Cell” under the supervision of the respective Deputy Commissioners across Sindh Province. In view of the said notification, learned counsel for the petitioner, upon being confronted, sought disposal of the petition, submitting that the petitioner intends to file a complaint before the Deputy Commissioner, Shaheed Benazirabad, for redressal of his grievance. Mr.Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, learned Additional Advocate General Sindh, who is present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice of the instant petition and concedes to the legal position stated herein.

In view of the said notification, learned counsel for the petitioner, upon being confronted, sought disposal of the petition, submitting that the petitioner intends to file a complaint before the Deputy Commissioner, Shaheed Benazirabad, for redressal of his grievance. Mr.Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, learned Additional Advocate General Sindh, who is present in Court in connection with other matters, waives notice of the instant petition and concedes to the legal position stated herein. 2 of 2 Accordingly, the present petition stands disposed of, leaving the petitioners at liberty to submit a proper application/complaint to the concerned Deputy Commissioner for redressal of their grievance. The said authority shall decide the matter, after providing an opportunity of hearing to all concerned, strictly in accordance with law, within thirty days from the date of receipt of this order. The office is directed to communicate a copy of this order to the learned A.A.G. for compliance. JUDGE JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
